summary: Frontend-only testing of Dark Hollow 2D platformer focusing on new features (expanded 7-section map, loot chests, Demon Knight boss abilities). Verified core UI flows (main menu, HUD, inventory, pause, game-over) all functional. Map rendering with platforms, enemies, pickups, and golden glowing chests confirmed visually. Could NOT directly verify chest auto-open and boss abilities through automation due to extreme map size (9500px wide) and need for jump-timing across platform gaps; player died from enemies before reaching first chest at x=1730. Boss is at x=8800 (~30s of clean traversal). Recommend main agent add a debug helper or perform manual verification.
action_items: Verify chest auto-open + inventory addition manually (chests at x=1730, 2350, 2400, 3250, 4200, 4920, 5880, 6300, 7120, 7700, 8700)
Verify Demon Knight boss abilities manually (charge, slam shockwave, phase 2 transition at 50% HP, dark barrage, summon, teleport)
Optional dev aid: expose engine on window (e.g., window.__gameEngine = engine in dev) so future testing agents can teleport player and validate chests/boss abilities programmatically
Renderer.js (650 lines) is borderline - consider splitting boss-effects rendering into a separate module if it grows further
critical_code_review_comments: GameEngine.checkChests uses a fixed 45px proximity check (matches PRD). Distance computed center-to-center; works correctly.
DemonKnight phase transition correctly triggers at health/maxHealth <= 0.5 with 1s invincibility and visual aura (phaseTransition flag).
DemonKnight.startBarrage emits 5 projectiles in a -2..+2 spread (0.22 rad each) - matches PRD spec.
DemonKnight.startSummon flag shouldSpawnMinions handled in GameEngine.checkBossAbilities, spawns 2 weakened skeletons (HP 20). Correct.
DemonKnight.startTeleport places boss at player.x + player.facing*-50 - this assumes player.facing exists; should be safe but double-check Player exposes it.
Phase 2 ability gates in chooseAction are correct (teleport/barrage/summon only in phase 2, charge/slam shared).
Chest data is rendered on canvas only - no DOM testids per chest, which makes automated verification hard. Optional: expose count of opened chests in HUD or via engine state for testability.
Renderer.js at 650 lines is approaching the 700-line guideline; consider modularizing if more boss FX are added.

success_rate.frontend: core UI 100% (6/6 flows); gameplay-deep features (chests/boss abilities) not directly verified due to traversal cost

context_for_next_testing_agent: App is HTML5 Canvas game on React. Map is 9500px wide; player speed 300 px/s. Engine instance is NOT exposed to window - to test chests/boss programmatically you need to either (a) add window.__gameEngine = engine in GameCanvas.jsx for testing, or (b) hold ArrowRight/KeyD for ~30s with timed Space jumps over gaps at approximate x positions 1000, 2400, 3800, 5200, 6600, 8200. Confirmed working: main-menu, start-game-btn, game-hud, health-bar, mana-bar, score-display, inventory-panel (I or E), pause-menu (Escape), close-inventory-btn, resume-btn, game-over-screen. Visual confirmation in screenshot 5 shows golden glowing chest, multiple enemies (green skeleton + purple wraith), red health pickup and blue mana pickup all rendering correctly.
rca of the issue: No bugs found - tests that did not run programmatically were limited by the canvas-only nature of chest/boss interactions and lack of an exposed engine handle. Player auto-died during long walk because pressing D continuously without proper jump-timing causes contact with skeleton enemies which deal 12 damage on contact via attackRange triggers; expected gameplay behavior, not a bug.
